assumptions: The intragroup dividend receivable is a monetary item between group entities with different functional currencies, so the related foreign exchange gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation.
Any hedge designation considered would also need to satisfy the formal designation, documentation and hedge effectiveness requirements in IFRS 9 paragraph 6.4.1.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 includes a fair value hedge model for a recognised asset or liability, and foreign currency risk on an intragroup monetary item can qualify in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 includes a cash flow hedge model for a recognised asset or liability, and the intragroup monetary item exception can make the foreign currency exposure hedgeable in consolidation.
